forecasts.reasoning: The border closures between Benin and Nigeria’s Kwara state (and extensions to other areas) were enacted in early May 2025 by Benin’s President Talon. The closures involve serious security concerns (the emergence of a new cross-border terrorist group called 'Mahmuda' and illicit arms smuggling) alongside trade disputes over agricultural commodities. As of the May 31, 2025 knowledge cutoff, there are no credible reports of an imminent reopening. The resolution date is July 1, 2025, leaving only a one-month window for the issue to be fully resolved. Historically, West African border closures, especially those tied to security threats and terror groups, take months or even years to resolve because establishing joint security patrols, neutralizing the threat, and formalizing diplomatic agreements moves slowly. Given the short remaining timeline and the severity of the stated reasons for the closure, it is highly unlikely the crossings will reopen before July 1.

The question is whether a UN member country will newly recognize Palestine in June 2025 (specifically between June 1 and July 1, 2025). 

From my search results, French President Emmanuel Macron has explicitly announced that France could recognize Palestine in June 2025, specifically tied to a UN conference on the Israeli-Palestinian conflict in New York (June 17–20). This conference is co-hosted by France and Saudi Arabia.
French PM François Bayrou and Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot recently (late May 2025) reaffirmed France's determination to recognize Palestine alongside the UK and Canada. 
The UK stated on April 30 and late May 2025 that it is in talks with France and Saudi Arabia for recognition, indicating that a joint move by at least France (and likely the UK and Canada) is highly probable. 
There is slight internal pushback; UK officials were quoted in late May (May 19) suspecting Macron might postpone the decision if facts on the ground in Gaza escalate, or waiting on a ceasefire. However, on May 20, France’s foreign minister explicitly condemned Israel's actions and stated France is "determined" to recognize it, alongside news from PM Bayrou confirming a joint effort with the UK and Canada. 

Given the explicit commitment of France (backed by UK and Canada) and the scheduled June 17-20 UN conference specifically designed as a rallying point for a two-state solution and Palestine's recognition, it is very likely that at least one UN member (such as France, UK, or Canada) will formally announce its recognition in June 2025. I will estimate the probability of this occurring at 77%. The main risk against this outcome is a last-minute delay or postponement of the conference or the announcement due to political/diplomatic complications.
